# Agent Guide

Read this file first. It tells you where to find context in this repo.

## Quick Reference

| What you need | Where to look |
|---|---|
| How this repo is structured | [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md) |
| How to build/test/run | [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) |
| Why decisions were made | [docs/ADRs/](./docs/ADRs/) |
| What this repo does | [README.md](./README.md) |
| PR review rules | [.bito/guidelines/](./.bito/guidelines/) |
| Active specs/work | [docs/specs/](./docs/specs/) |
| Migration guide (v11 to v12) | [MIGRATION.md](./MIGRATION.md) |

## Sharp Edges & Invariants

- **Never use deep imports** — all public API must come from the package root (`contentful-management`). The `exports` field in `package.json` enforces this. Internal paths are not part of the public API and will break without notice.
- **Never remove or rename a publicly exported type without a major version** — downstream consumers rely on type imports; even type-only changes are breaking.
- **Always use `type` imports for type-only symbols** — ESLint enforces `@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports: error`. Use `import type { Foo }` not `import { Foo }` when `Foo` is only used as a type.
- **The plain client is the primary API** — the legacy (nested) client is deprecated. New endpoints should only be added to `lib/adapters/REST/endpoints/` and `lib/plain/plain-client-types.ts`. Do not add new methods to `lib/create-*-api.ts` files.
- **Entity types and REST endpoints are 1:1** — every entity in `lib/entities/` has a corresponding file in `lib/adapters/REST/endpoints/`. Keep them in sync.
- **semantic-release controls versioning** — never manually bump `package.json` version. The version field (`0.0.0-determined-by-semantic-release`) is replaced at publish time. The `test:version` script verifies this placeholder is NOT in the built output.
- **Integration tests hit the real CMA** — they require `CONTENTFUL_INTEGRATION_TEST_CMA_TOKEN` and `CONTENTFUL_ORGANIZATION_ID`. They run in CI but are difficult to run locally. Focus on unit tests during development.
- **No `package-lock.json` changes in feature PRs** — lock file changes should be isolated to dedicated dependency update PRs (Dependabot or manual).

## Key Conventions

- **Commit format:** [Angular Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) via semantic-release. Types: `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `docs`, `refactor`, `test`, `perf`, `ci`, `build`. Use `feat!:` or `BREAKING CHANGE` footer for major bumps.
- **Branch strategy:** `master` is the primary branch. Release branches: `beta`, `canary`, `dev`, `*.x` (maintenance). Feature branches merged via PR.
- **Test location:** `test/unit/` mirrors `lib/` structure. Integration tests in `test/integration/`. Type tests co-located in `lib/**/*.test-d.ts`.
- **Module system:** Dual ESM + CJS. Source is TypeScript compiled via Rollup. ESM output uses `.mjs` extension, CJS uses `.cjs`.
- **Formatting:** Prettier (single quotes, no semicolons, 100 char width). Enforced by pre-commit hook (husky + lint-staged).

## Integration Points

**Upstream (this repo wraps):**
- Contentful CMA REST API (`api.contentful.com`)
- Contentful Upload API (`upload.contentful.com`)

**Downstream (consumes this repo):**
- `contentful-cli` — CLI tool for Contentful operations
- `@contentful/app-sdk` — App Framework SDK (uses CMA adapter)
- `contentful-migration` — content model migration tool
- Contentful Web App
- Customer Node.js and browser applications

## Build & Quality

```bash
# Quick verification loop
npm ci && npm run build && npm run test:unit:cover && npm run lint && npm run format:check
```
